danger_signals.visual_form: Pointed, metallic protrusions on platforms and ceilings
danger_signals.meaning: Instant damage or death on contact; impassable without special protection

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, ornate mechanical enemy sprite with distinct color palette and design
danger_signals.meaning: Boss-level threat; stage culmination; requires weaponry mastery to defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Shadowed or darkened Robot Master-like sprites with menacing appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Deceptive threat; imposter enemies designed to frame Proto Man; escalating danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Metal platforms, industrial architecture, heavy mechanical theming
danger_signals.meaning: Wily's stronghold; highest-difficulty content; final confrontation zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Small animated sprites (missiles, tentacles, energy blasts) moving toward Mega Man
danger_signals.meaning: Ranged threat; requires dodging or blocking; indicates enemy aggression

safety_signals.visual_form: Text dialogue and character appearance at game start
safety_signals.meaning: Mission briefing; creator authority; establishes legitimate objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Flying bird companion sprite following Mega Man
safety_signals.meaning: Ally asset; damage multiplier; visual confirmation of power-up acquisition

safety_signals.visual_form: Small glowing items (red/blue) dropped by defeated enemies or found in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration; resource availability; enemy defeat confirmation

safety_signals.visual_form: Text-based communication during stage selection and briefing
safety_signals.meaning: Trusted ally; legitimate guidance; continuity with prior game events

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual effect and UI indicator when Robot Master defeated
safety_signals.meaning: Progress confirmation; tactical advantage unlocked; stage completion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mega Buster arm cannon; stylized 'M' logo
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Clean, orderly laboratory aesthetic in briefing sequences

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Whistle as audio signature; distinctive cloak/robe design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark Man imposters wear shadowed or corrupted versions of Proto Man's silhouette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Proto Man's Castle (fortress stage design with prototypical mechanical aesthetic)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Castle architecture; mechanical fortress design; evil scientist trappings
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark Men robots represent Wily's secret creation; Robot Masters are his proxies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wily's Castle stages feature heavy metal plating, spikes, and industrial machinery; multi-stage fortress layout

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied per master (Star Man: yellow/white; Stone Man: gray/brown; Wave Man: blue/teal; Gravity Man: purple; Gyro Man: orange; Crystal Man: cyan; Charge Man: yellow; Napalm Man: orange/red)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual weapon signatures (e.g., Wave Man's water attacks, Stone Man's gravity; Gravity Man's gravity manipulation)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each master has unique sprite design reflecting their element/function
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Each stage environment matches the Robot Master's thematic identity (e.g., Stone Man's stage has rocky/metallic platforms; Wave Man's stage has water/aquatic theming)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Proto Man kidnapped Dr. Light (apparent crisis state)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen and opening narrative; dialogue from Dr. Cossack explaining the situation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mega Man must traverse stages to confront Robot Masters; no option to refuse mission; urgency conveyed through narrative tone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage environments feature Robot Master-specific hazards and enemy types; boss sprite appears at stage end with distinct appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies attack on sight; stages escalate in difficulty; Robot Master uses special weapon attacks; player must defeat boss to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual effect on boss defeat; weapon acquisition UI appears; stage transitions to weapon copy animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains access to Master Weapon; stage no longer presents challenge; progression marker confirmed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle architecture introduces visual shift from varied Robot Master stages to unified fortress aesthetic; darker color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dark Man encounters replace Robot Masters; deception mechanic engaged; player must identify imposters through visual inspection

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final revelation: Wily orchestrated everything (story twist)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dialogue exposition; Wily Castle stage introduction; fortress redesign with heavy mechanical theming
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final bosses include remaining Dark Men plus Wily himself; fortress self-destructs during escape sequence; visual urgency conveyed through collapsing architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Proto Man appears in distant silhouette at fortress collapse; whistle audio cue; ceiling is blasted away to allow escape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Proto Man rescues Mega Man and Dr. Light without direct confrontation; 'quietly slips away unnoticed' suggests autonomy and hidden heroism

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage design reflecting Robot Master identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Stone Man's stage features rocky platforms and metallic surfaces suggesting industrial/mining environment; Wave Man's stage includes water hazards and aquatic theming

environmental_storytelling.technique: Color coding for faction and threat level
environmental_storytelling.example: Robot Masters use distinct primary colors (Star Man: yellow, Crystal Man: cyan); Dark Men use dark/corrupted palette to signal deception
Proto Man's Castle and Wily's Castle use heavy metal plating and spike hazards to visually communicate enemy stronghold; multi-stage layout suggests increasing containment/security
Each Robot Master stage includes thematic enemy types (e.g., water enemies in Wave Man stage); Wily Castle includes mixture of all enemy types indicating synthesis of threat
Early stages have manageable spike placement; Wily Castle stages escalate spike density and lethal platform arrangements; fortress collapse sequence uses environmental destruction as visual climax

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character appearance signaling allegiance and intent
environmental_storytelling.example: Proto Man's dark cloak and silhouette distinguish him from Mega Man's blue suit; Dark Men's shadowed appearance creates visual dissonance before deception is revealed

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stands ready; responds to Dr. Light's commands; accepts objective without hesitation
Runs, jumps, shoots continuously; charges Mega Buster; collects dropped weapons; avoids hazards; defeats enemies for resources
Adapts tactics; switches between Master Weapons and Mega Buster; responds to boss attack patterns; persists despite damage
Waits at stage end; sprite is static/idle; represents territorial control
Attacks with element-specific weapon; follows movement patterns; retreats/advances based on health; displays visual damage states
Explosion animation; removal from game world; weapon transfer to Mega Man
Visually appears as corrupted Robot Master; attacks aggressively; reveals identity only after defeat or via narrative exposition
Orchestrates Robot Master attacks; remains unseen until late game; audio cue (whistle) signals presence without visual confirmation until finale
Intervenes to save Mega Man; demonstrates agency independent of Wily; 'quietly slips away' suggests autonomous moral choice
Controllers Dark Man robots; uses Proto Man impersonation as cover; retreats after defeat; avoids direct confrontation until final castle
Emerge from terrain or move in predictable patterns; attack on sight; drop resources when defeated; indicate stage hazard level through density

notes: Mega Man 5 uses classic NES-era visual communication: primary color identity for heroes (blue), dark/contrasting colors for deception (Dark Men), stage theming to reflect enemy identity, and environmental hazard density to signal boss proximity and danger escalation. The game's narrative twist (Proto Man framed, Wily orchestrating) is entirely supported by visual design: the Dark Men's corrupted appearance, Wily Castle's industrial fortress aesthetic, and the final rescue via Proto Man's whistle and silhouette. The faction system is simple but clear: Light's team (blue/clean), Wily's visible agents (Robot Masters, varied colors per theme), Wily's hidden agents (Dark Men, shadowed), and the falsely-accused Proto Man (black/dark but heroic). Beat's presence as a flying companion provides visual-behavioral confirmation of progress and power acquisition. No numeric indicators for danger are necessary; hazard placement, enemy density, and sprite size/complexity communicate threat level.
